title: Copyright trolling kept evidence of Japanese cult classic Cookie's Bustle offline, until now
summary: The evil is defeated.

title: How many of these MMORPGs can you name in just 6 minutes based on a handful of vague details?
summary: A challenge for the eternally online.

title: Highguard gets a new playable character, skill trees and more ahead of next week's closure
summary: Wildlight's skeleton crew is pumping out material while it still can.

title: Hunt: Showdown keeps experimenting with the extraction genre, and its next twist sounds irresistible: Soon, extraction points and loot won't appear on the map
summary: The Devil's Trail begins March 18.

title: US government reportedly debating whether to force Tencent to give up its US-based game holdings
summary: The process began during the first Trump administration, and is apparently now picking up steam.

title: More than 800 gamers took an exam to prove they could complete an '80s adventure game without peeking at a walkthrough—and only 2 passed
summary: Only .25% of players completed the AGAT, the Adventure Game Aptitude Test, designed by fiendish developer Woe Industries.

title: Stop the clock: Resident Evil Requiem's first sexy Leon mod hit Nexus Mods just 4 days, 11 hours, and 23 minutes after launch
summary: I'm glad we can all share this moment.

title: MindsEye studio claims it now has 'overwhelming evidence of organized espionage and corporate sabotage' that somehow made its game bad, and also it's laying off more people
summary: This would all be kind of amusing if it wasn't for the facts that more game developers are losing their jobs.

title: Resident Evil Requiem's 5 million sales have made it one of Capcom's all-time top 20 bestsellers in just 4 days
summary: Call it the Leon bump. (Or don't call it that).

title: Counter-Strike: Global Offensive is back: 3 years after it was muscled out by Counter-Strike 2, CS:GO has its own Steam page again and has powered its way back onto Steam's most-played chart
summary: CS:GO was subsumed into Counter-Strike 2 a few years ago, but now it has its own Steam page again.

title: Deus Ex: Invisible War wasn't what it should have been because the studio moved to an engine that was really built for Thief: 'A super-boneheaded call, very bad decision… it really tanked development'
summary: "I wish we could redo that project and just stitch the maps together."

title: A 9,999,999 gold painting in World of Warcraft: Midnight sent secret finders on a hunt for the ultimate discount and one thrifty player finally figured it out
summary: Players will do anything for housing decor.

title: Finally someone has threaded the needle between survival game and horse girl sim
summary: The Legend of Khiimori is a surprisingly detailed horse courier sim that I'm actually loving more than just in solidarity with my horse girl gamer friends.

title: Ubisoft finally confirms the Assassin's Creed 4: Black Flag remake is real, and it's dropping the '4' for some reason
summary: "Keep your spyglass on the horizon."

title: The devs on Deus Ex: Invisible War knew its most-hated part was a 'terrible idea,' and they give the director 'sh*t about it all the time'
summary: Some choices need a reload.

title: Qualcomm's new Snapdragon X2 Arm CPU pops up in Geekbench and wallops the x86 laptop competition by over 30% in single-core performance
summary: How much this translates into gaming is very much another question.

title: Highguard director shares stats and thoughts on the game's failure, denies conspiracy theories about where it all went wrong: 'Nobody will know the true story of the studio or game'
summary: Highguard did better than it might appear, but still nowhere near well enough to be sustainable.

title: As Assassin's Creed Shadows support comes to an end, Ubisoft reiterates what's coming in the future, and yes, co-op factors into it somehow
summary: Also, everything you've heard about the PvP project is a lie.

title: As hardware prices make heads spin, Apple of all companies has just announced a new MacBook laptop for only $599
summary: I don't think you're ready for the takedown.

title: Meta's new FrameSync feature aims to make VR a little less nauseating
summary: As performance demands grow for Quest headsets, Meta's updating its frame timing technique.

title: Indie dev says 'best I can do is sentient tofu' after fans demand a male protagonist—they respond oh yeah, actually Mr Tofu works fine, thanks
summary: Everyone got what they wanted. Kind of.

title: South Korean authorities seized $5.6 million worth of crypto then accidentally posted pictures of the super-secret recovery phrase, resulting in the loss of... $4.8 million worth of crypto
summary: Aaaand it's gone. Mostly.

title: Overwatch's grand revamp event is a suffocating blip in an otherwise successful return to form
summary: Conquest should be fun, but it's more frustrating.

title: AMD's CPU division is booming as CEO Dr. Lisa Su says sales 'far exceeded my expectations'
summary: And yes, of course, it's down to AI.

title: Arc Raiders devs had to nerf the blueprint drop rate because players are too good at finding them: 'First Wave caches have been handing out a lot of blueprints'
summary: Wish that was me smh.

title: After planning 2 more paid acts, narrative scifi power-washer Ambrosia Sky is instead finishing its story in one free update to 'maximize' its focus on what players loved: 'People got in on that sapphic yearning right away'
summary: So much for slow burn.

title: Kiss goodbye to Ghost of Yotei on PC: Sony is U-turning on its 'let's put our games on PC' strategy and bunkering back down with console exclusives, says Bloomberg
summary: Nothing gold can stay.

title: Marathon roadmap: All upcoming updates
summary: New Runners, weapons, gear, and maps.

title: Control Resonant is far more of an RPG than I expected, complete with talent trees, stats screens, and build-crafting that wouldn't feel out of place in a Diablo game
summary: Remedy's taking another ambitious creative swing with its new sequel.

title: Overwatch is getting a bunch of Nier: Automata skins and at this point I'm convinced support mains are the only ones spending money on this game
summary: Another Kiriko and Mercy skin? Say it ain't so.
